programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sonia Woodley is stabbed two years after the death of her husband.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Ford Florey is a town with a Wild West Society and many grudges. During a Wild West show at the local faire, the witch on the 'Dunk the Witch' stall is well and truly dunked but laughter soon turns to horror when she doesn't get up and the water in the tank starts to turn red. Barnaby and Jones need to be quick on the draw to track down the murderer, and soon begin to wonder whether a dispute over land was the motive, or something far more sinister, especially when more people are killed, with methods linked to the Wild West.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: On a dark night in the village of March Magna, Barnaby's wife Joyce swerves her car and narrowly misses a shadowy figure in the road - or so she thinks, when later that night a body is discovered in the old cemetery. The victim was a member of the council who was strange and weird, but someone seemed to have quite a grudge on him, and so Barnaby and Jones delve into his background, and the village, attempting to determine who might have wanted him dead, finding ghosts, grudges, and more beneath the village's surface.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Piano student Zoe Stock has won a place at the Devington Manor Winter School led by internationally renowned musician, Sir Michael Fielding (James Fox). Yet when she is there, things become mysterious when from the river bank in the grounds of the manor she sees a woman jump from the bridge and disappear underwater. When Barnaby and Jones start investigating the possible drowning, they discover unsavoury connections to the past that could prove lethal 20 years later, especially when someone attempts to kill. It isn't long, before murder does occur, and the detectives attempt to fathom what occurred in the drowning, and the present day murders.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Midsomer Morchard's very own boxer, John Kinsella, wins a world title in New York, the success seems to be a joyous time for all, until murder rears its ugly head. When a local solicitor is found dead by his assistant and Kinsella's manager, Barnaby attempts to determine what is going on, and soon discover issues with inheritance, affairs, anger, and gambling to make matters difficult. Meanwhile Kinsella's trainer Teddy Molloy (Phil Daniels) has his own hidden agenda. When a re-enactment of a Victorian boxing match gets heated, and Kinsella's manager is found dead in his home's gym, the pair attempt to determine who is responsible, although Barnaby's judgement seems to be clouded on this one.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute turns to murder, after a leading light in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe - but when a member of the possible scam is themselves murdered, Barnaby wonders what is truly going on behind the scenes of an important by-election.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: La troca de Sinaloa
programme.desc: Julián dejó la policía para tener una vida pacífica con su familia, pero cuando una pandilla asesina a su familia, Julián decide vengarse.

programme.title: Atrapados en Michoacán
programme.desc: Félix y su inseparable amigo el Marrano van a Michoacán a hacer negocios y recoger un cargamento valioso, pero en su camino se meten en muchos problemas.

programme.title: Ladies Nice
programme.desc: Las vidas de tres jóvenes arrogantes y de clase alta se trastocan cuando atropellan accidentalmente a un niño y se enfrentan a la realidad.

programme.title: Despedida de Narcos

programme.desc: Un valet roba la identidad de un moribundo para cambiar su vida, pero, sin saberlo, su nueva oportunidad podría llevarlo a la ruina.

programme.desc: En Juárez desaparecen mujeres. En la mira está Julián Cantú, un comerciante con doble personalidad. Montoya, su asistente, es sospechoso.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Fevzi lleva a Öyku a casa de Metin. Al verlos, Melissa se emociona y corre a abrazarlos, pero su tía la regaña. Defne encuentra un objeto extraño en el escritorio de su padre. Melissa le cuenta a Defne lo que le sucedió a Öyku.

programme.title: Destinos cruzados
programme.desc: Toprak despierta de un coma tras cinco años sin recordar nada de su pasado, y regresa a una familia que no reconoce, incluida su prometida de toda la vida y un niño que vive con él. Sin embargo, su amnesia lo mantiene en una incertidumbre constante.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Después de tantos años Jade y Lucas finalmente están juntos, ella acepta hacer el amor con él y se aman durante toda la noche sin importarles nada. Dora encuentra a su hijo Daniel.

programme.title: Eres mi estrella
programme.desc: La compañía que maneja el grupo ídolo ANJELL insiste en contratar un nuevo cantante porque la voz del cantante principal Tae Kyung, ya no es la misma.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Juvenile Lifers: Road to Redemption

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Massachusetts has a long history of land conservation. Now local nonprofits, volunteers, and advocates are leading the charge to “re-wild” forests – returning them to their natural condition while protecting biodiverse habitats. We visit Mass Audubon’s Tidmarsh Wildlife Sanctuary in Plymouth, and see how the Native Land Conservancy is restoring parts of the Muddy Pond Preserve in Kingston. At Walden Pond, high schoolers work to understand Henry David Thoreau’s message of self-reliance and sustainability. We also explore how a local land trust is using conservation land to help feed families who are food-insecure.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Shift: The Ragbrai Documentary
programme.desc: A celebration of the oldest, largest, and longest recreational touring bicycle ride in the world. Three riders and a pair of community leaders reach new personal heights, changing their lives along the seven day ride across Iowa.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: “Paintings” that are actually made of throwaway plastic. A bakery where they’re jumping in on the “Is It Cake?” trend. And a museum based on illusions. Tonight, a journey of the mind, which can trick us into thinking one thing is actually another.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Cold temps and cabin fever got you down? Stick an umbrella in your drink and join us for a return trip to some of Chronicle’s island destinations. Anthony explores some of the 365 beaches on the island of Antigua (one for every day of the year). Shayna Seymour climbs aboard a catamaran to sail the waters around Barbados. And Ted Reinstein finds a little R&R right nearby in Bermuda. New England beach weather may be six months off but this island tour may help tide you over!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Saddle up! Chronicle is heading west! Anthony Everett brings us inside the world of fall in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, and the nearby Grand Tetons. After a trail ride on horseback at one of the area’s many dude ranches, Anthony takes a seat on a saddle at the Million Dollar Cowboy Bar. He meets a pair of brothers, transplants from Cape Cod who have carved out a career making action sports films. Craving more adventure? Anthony and crew encounter “snowliage” during a snow-filled (yes, snow-filled) October mountain climb. He samples the world’s “highest waffles” and finds handmade cowboy hats in case you’re in need of a souvenir.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Danny’s a family man now and needs to upgrade his smile, damaged by past drug use. But he’s been burned by dentists before. Tattoo artist Craig claims that no one’s been able to give him teeth he won’t break. Will their faith in dentists be restored?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Wormtown, Shovel Town, Whip City - Massachusetts folks love a nickname, and we’re back for Round 2 of testing your nickname knowledge. It’s a journey through town monikers that are less obvious than the Witch City (Salem).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Stories of everyday New Englanders doing extraordinary things in their own “Labors of Love.” Ted Reinstein revisits Boston’s old West End with a man pushing to memorialize one of the neighborhood’s native sons -- the legendary actor, Leonard Nimoy. Ted hops a sailboat with Sail Cape Cod which is helping those with challenges find a bit of respite on the open waters. He treks to the Cape Ann Museum where they’re honoring Gloucester fishing families through art. Legos to preserve history? It’s happening in Rhode Island. Finally, Ted also finds free bikes in Lowell for those who need them.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is back with more stories of people pursuing their passions. Ted meets a man who is devoted to keeping the memory of fallen Massachusetts veterans alive. He later reconnects with the couple working to restore the cabin of a pioneering author and early civil rights figure.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is back with all new stories of New Englanders following their passions.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Brett Hawkes’ journey of discovery took him from the North Shore of Massachusetts to Northern France, where he bicycled on the path his grandfather marched through World War I. Tonight, Ted Reinstein brings us the moving story of this local man determined to get to know his grandfather, long after his passing. Also tonight, Ted meets a local couple on a mission to restore a long-neglected Jewish cemetery in Poland; a mother-and-son on a mission to help revive a community in the Berkshires with the reopening of its general store; and a Rhode Islander on a mission to give sanctuary to exotic birds.

programme.title: FIXT

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: She was known as the “Lady of the Dunes,” and her identity remained a mystery for 48 years, until just this past Halloween. Chronicle has been following the case for decades and tonight Anthony Everett revisits the investigation into the remains of a woman found in the remote dunes of Provincetown. Anthony looks into just who victim Ruth Marie Terry was, speaks with law enforcement officials who never gave up the search for her identity, and examines the investigation that now includes the bizarre history of Terry’s husband. A look into where this investigation stands after nearly five decades of work.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Lake Sunapee is sometimes overshadowed by the bigger lakes in New Hampshire, but it has long been a draw for summer vacationers seeking peace and quiet, outdoor activity, and scenic New England charm. Tonight Ted Reinstein is cruising the Sunapee Scenic Byway, a 39-mile route that winds through the towns of Sunapee, Newbury, and New London. In Sunapee Harbor he steps back in time with a cruise aboard the steamboat M.V. Kearsarge and in Newbury he explores the historic John Hay Estate. And there’s no snow on the ground, but Mount Sunapee is still buzzing with activity.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Summer gets most of the attention in the Lakes Region of New Hampshire, but in spring Chronicle’s fancy turns to thoughts of…farms! We stop by a “micro” dairy, meet two generations of a Sanbornton family holding onto their beloved 19th century farm, and Shayna Seymour takes in spectacular views of Winnipesaukee’s smaller sibling, Lake Winnisquam. Shayna also stops by a winery (yes, they do grow grapes in the Granite State) and checks out the views from Winnisquam’s only lakefront B&B.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we celebrate local women who lead and inspire. We begin with women leading in the food biz, from legendary Boston chef Jody Adams to the team behind the “Tres Latinas” food truck. Shayna Seymour chats with broadcast journalist Katie Couric about her new venture promoting female-led, mission-driven companies (including locals Line in the Sand and Rafi Nova). And we’re taking a ride with Women on Wheels, the brainchild of a local woman who champions cycling for women.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They are the glass-ceiling breakers and problem-solvers - local women inspiring change and making an impact. Tonight, Erika Tarantal introduces us to the first female commander of the U.S.S Constitution, and we visit a local film studio believed to be one of the only woman-owned and operated in the country. A health problem pushed the owner of Nüssli to take charge and create nourishing sweet treats, while the search for the perfect workwear inspired the entrepreneur behind Spencer Jane. And basketball in a hijab? Shayna Seymour plays ball with the local player and coach shattering stereotypes and expectations with her storied career.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, stories about women making their mark in business, sports, and the arts. Erika Tarantal talks to players, coaches, and owners (and even hits the ice herself!).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we meet Marina Mabrey, shooting guard for the Connecticut Sun. Shayna Seymour chats with Robin Roberts, the popular anchor of ABC’s Good Morning America. Also we go inside Rosie’s Place, celebrating its 50th year caring for women in need.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We connect with women leading the way in sports, music, and business, including Erika Tarantal’s one-on-one with cosmetics mogul Bobbi Brown to talk about her new makeup line.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Massachusetts is home to more than 300,000 military veterans, and tonight we talk to several about the challenges and the opportunities after completing service. We meet a Marine who discovered a therapeutic outlet in gardening, and with his family launched a community garden to help other vets. We also visit veteran-owned businesses and meet those who are committed to honoring fallen heroes. Finally, we talk with a 100-year-old World War II vet who shares memories of his experiences.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Erika Tarantal takes us to Beverly, recently voted "Hottest 2025 Zip Code,” to see what all the fuss is about.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It boasts one of America’s best Main Streets, according to one travel guide, so Anthony Everett is checking out Littleton, N.H., once a factory town and now home to local residents committed to maintaining its vibrancy. Anthony stops by the original Lahout’s ski shop, which lays claim to being the nation’s oldest, and takes a ride along Parker Mountain Trails on a “fat bike.” We visit Mount Eustis – a local ski hill where the community has come together to keep the place going – and sample the offerings at family-owned White Mountains Canning.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Palaces aren’t just for royals. Tonight we’re exploring New England’s version of castles and other estates. We meet the artist who snapped up the Searles Castle in Great Barrington, a former boarding school turned private residence. In Orange, a massive effort to restore a beloved local property is underway, thanks to a Californian who wanted to change up her life. Juggler Meadow in Leverett is the house that Yankee Candle built. Finally, a visit to the ruins of the legendary Madame Cheri’s Castle in Chesterfield, N.H.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan heads out in search of things “Lost and Found.” She sets off on a treasure hunt around Boston, armed with a metal detector. Finally, Nicole meets people who experienced a loss of health and stability – and through social media, “found” a connection to community.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New Englanders dream all fall, winter, and spring about summertime. And when the temperature finally goes up, we start searching for ways to cool down! From a summer concert shed on Webster Lake to ‘Busta Rhymes Island’ – tonight we discover there is lots to *lake* about New England.
